commits.author: usavsolutionsinc
commits.subject: Make receiving idempotent against retries + concurrent double-fire (BUG-0013/0014)
commits.body:
BUG-0013: receiveLineUnits now short-circuits on a batch-level idempotency
guard — if this client_event_id's per-unit events already exist on the line
(committed by a prior call), return the current state without mutating. The old
per-unit ordinal suffix (`:unit-N`, N from the moving quantity_received) shifted
on retry, so ON CONFLICT(client_event_id) missed and the counter + non-serial
ledger double-applied; only mark-received-po's qty clamp masked it. Now
idempotent for any caller; concurrent dups block on the FOR UPDATE lock then
short-circuit.

BUG-0014: add reserve-up-front idempotency (claimOrReplay / finalize /
release) so two concurrent requests with the same Idempotency-Key can't both
run produce() (which double-called Zoho purchase-receive). The first claims a
pending row; a concurrent second replays the finished response or gets 409
in-progress. Abandoned claims self-heal after a stale window. Additive — the
existing get/save helpers are unchanged (zero blast radius on the other ~34
routes); adopted in mark-received-po at its existing claim/finalize/catch spots.
DB-free unit test covers proceed/replay/in-progress/stale-reclaim/release/5xx.

commits.author: usavsolutionsinc
commits.subject: fix(packing-logs): write pack audit on the tenant txn client via recordAudit
commits.body:
The matched-order and not-found paths wrote the PACK_COMPLETED audit row
with the shared `pool` connection while the station_activity_logs row was
created on the open withTenantTransaction `client`. The uncommitted SAL row
wasn't visible to the separate connection, so the
audit_logs.station_activity_log_id FK violated (23503) and rolled back the
whole pack — surfacing as a 500 on POST /api/packing-logs.

Switch both calls to recordAudit(client, ctx, req, …): runs in the same
transaction (FK resolves) and never throws (audit failures are logged and
dropped), so a future audit hiccup can't roll back a completed pack. Keeps
the historical uppercase entityType literals to preserve audit-dashboard
continuity; action uses AUDIT_ACTION.PACK_COMPLETED (identical value).

commits.body:

the SHIPPED event go through transition(input, client, ctx.organizationId) — which
also fixes the previously org-unscoped serial UPDATE. The PACKED/LABELED audit
events + the sku_stock_ledger SOLD decrement are untouched (the money side does
not move through transition).

Allow-list: add FBA direct-ship edges STOCKED/GRADED/TESTED→SHIPPED (ALLOCATED/
PICKED→SHIPPED already existed) — FBA ships sellable units straight to Amazon
(link-unit normally allocates first). A non-sellable source (IN_REPAIR/SCRAPPED/…)
is NOT modeled and is now correctly rejected rather than force-shipped (a latent
bug — you should never ship a broken unit to FBA).

Review-driven hardening: pre-validate ALL units against the guard BEFORE mutating
any, returning a 409 listing every unshippable unit — true all-or-nothing with
clear observability, instead of an opaque mid-loop 500. (Idempotency is preserved
by the existing `current_status <> 'SHIPPED'` query filter, which excludes
already-shipped units on retry.)

Unconditional transition()-only (SoT hook forbids a raw fallback). +guard tests.

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: feat(inventory): route returns-intake through guarded transition() (Phase 1.3)
commits.body:
processReturnsIntake no longer hand-writes serial_units→RETURNED + a manual
RETURNED INSERT. The status change + the RETURNED event (scan_token=tracking,
stock_ledger_id=ledger, rich payload all preserved) go through transition()
(executor pattern on the tx client, org-scoped via input.organizationId). The
allocation flip (SHIPPED→RETURNED) and the +1 RETURN_CUSTOMER ledger stay outside
it. SHIPPED/STOCKED/RMA→RETURNED are modeled; an unmodeled source throws and rolls

Adversarial review of the parts-sort conversion found an atomicity regression:
transition() committed the STOCKED status+event in its own tx, then the
current_location move ran as a separate autocommit — a failure between them left
the unit STOCKED but not relocated. Wrap both in ONE transaction (the caller's
client when provided, else our own via neon-client transaction()), so status +
event + location commit together.

Also narrow transition()'s db param from PoolClient to Pick<PoolClient,'query'>
(the executor path only uses .query) — removes the unsafe cast in parts-sort and
lets any caller thread a partial tx client without one. Backward-compatible: a
full PoolClient still satisfies the narrower type.

(GRADED/RECEIVED/TESTED/RETURNED → STOCKED) are guard-clean; an odd source
(e.g. mid-repair) is declined → returns {sorted:false, reason:'blocked'} +

UPDATEs). holdUnit + releaseUnit no longer hand-write serial_units.current_status
+ a manual inventory_events INSERT — they go through the guarded transition()
chokepoint on the helper's own tx client (executor pattern). transition() emits
the HELD / RELEASED_HOLD event itself.

- state-machine: model ON_HOLD's outgoing edges as exactly hold.ts'
  RESTORABLE_STATUSES, so guarded release never rejects (entry to ON_HOLD stays
  universal via the to===ON_HOLD special-case). +guard tests.
- events: add HELD / RELEASED_HOLD to InventoryEventType (additive — already in

NOTE — deviation from the flag-gated pattern, forced by the source-of-truth hook
(.claude/hooks/sot-guard.sh): the rule forbids ANY raw current_status UPDATE in a
file, so a flag-OFF raw fallback is impossible. This conversion is therefore
UNCONDITIONAL (not behind UNIFIED_ENGINE_APPLY_TRANSITION). It is behavior-
equivalent for legitimate flows: hold's to=ON_HOLD is universal-entry and
release's destination is always a RESTORABLE_STATUSES member (now modeled), so

serial_units UPDATE + a separate non-atomic inventory_event + a receiving_lines
write, all over the BYPASSRLS owner pool with no org scoping and no tap.

  legacy serial UPDATE, inventory_event) — closes the same pre-existing
  cross-tenant gap the review flagged elsewhere. Byte-identical for legitimate
  single-org traffic when the flag is off.

  call sites) so skipTap callers don't pass a misleading placeholder event.

commits.subject: fix(workflow): applyTransition idempotency must not swallow expectedFrom drift
commits.body:
transition() runs the expectedFrom drift check BEFORE the guard's identity
check, so with optimistic concurrency a 409 — even one where from===to — is a
drift rejection the caller asked to fail on, not an idempotent re-entry. Only
treat from===to as an idempotent re-entry when no expectedFrom was supplied;
otherwise surface the 409 (no event, no tap). recordTestVerdict doesn't use
expectedFrom, so this is a latent-edge hardening for future callers + a test.

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: feat(workflow): applyTransition() mutate-and-tap chokepoint (Phase 1.1)
commits.body:
The single helper that collapses load→decide→guard→write-status→record-event→
tap (the ~26-handler shotgun). It COMPOSES the pieces that already exist rather
than reinventing them:
  transition()  -> FOR UPDATE · guard · UPDATE serial_units · inventory_event (atomic, org-aware)
  tapWorkflow() -> fire-and-forget engine observe / graph advance

- Idempotent re-entry: from===to (transition's 'identity' 409) still records the
  event (client_event_id de-dupes) and taps -> idempotent:true. Genuine guard
  rejections (409) / not-found (404) do NOT tap.
- Collaborators injected (default = real impls) so it's unit-tested with fakes.

Guard widening (state-machine.ts): add the real test-bench edges recordTestVerdict
performs so PASS/TEST_AGAIN flow THROUGH the guard instead of a raw UPDATE —
IN_TEST→TESTED (the PASS happy path), REPAIR_DONE→TESTED, TESTED→IN_TEST. Resolves
the brief-divergence in favor of TESTED (the established pass state; condition
GRADED is a separate axis). Additive = strictly more permissive, no caller affected.

Tests: applyTransition (happy/identity/reject/404) + guard allow-list edges.

but nothing reset them — errored units silently died. Adds the recovery path:

  Live lens only counts in aggregate.

  scan away) so a floor lead can unpark without graph-edit rights.

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: feat(workflow): real per-unit AdvanceLock replacing NULL_LOCK (Phase 1.0)
commits.body:
Best-effort Upstash Redis mutex keyed wf:advance:{serialUnitId}, wired into
the production advance() path. Mirrors api-guard.ts's REST /pipeline shape.

Failure policy (the lock narrows races; engine idempotency is the backstop):
- Redis unconfigured -> acquire() true / release() no-op (== NULL_LOCK), so
  dev/CI/preview are unaffected.
- SET NX miss (already held) -> acquire() false -> 2nd concurrent advance noops.
- Redis error/timeout -> acquire() true (fail OPEN), never stalls a tap.

Short TTL auto-expires a crashed holder; release is a token-checked
compare-and-delete (Lua) so we never free a lock a later advance re-acquired.

The in-app updater never updated because (a) the version never changed and

requires. This wires up a real, verifiable auto-update feed.

  the Apple secrets (CSC_LINK, CSC_KEY_PASSWORD, APPLE_ID,
  APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID) are added to the repo.
- Observability: route electron-updater through electron-log so the update
  lifecycle (checking/available/downloaded/error) is written to main.log.

commits.author: usavsolutionsinc
commits.subject: feat(receiving): derive delivery from physical dock scan when carrier lags
commits.body:
Carrier "delivered" is absent or late for most inbound boxes: of 1,291 scanned
cartons, 728 were scanned (707 fully opened) while the carrier still said NOT
delivered — USPS access-blocked/NULL-status (540), unknown-carrier numbers that
never poll (165), FedEx stale (22); when it does land the lag runs to ~87 days
for USPS. The physical scan is the real delivery truth.

Add a 'receiving_scan' pass to runReconcileDeliveredJob: for an inbound shipment
matched by the same last-8 / shipment-link rule the Incoming display uses, set
is_delivered + is_terminal, stamp the earliest matching scan as delivered_at,
and stop polling. Gated by INBOUND_SHIPMENT_PREDICATE so an outbound order
shipment whose tracking last-8 coincidentally collides with a scan is never
flipped (warranty-safe). Extract SHIPMENT_SCAN_MATCH_CONDITION so the EXISTS
predicate and the delivered_at derivation share one match definition.

Scanned boxes are excluded from delivered-unscanned by construction, so flipping
is_delivered cannot resurface them (verified: 0 receiving_scan rows in the
delivered-unscanned base). Backfilled 766 inbound shipments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: derive delivery from physical dock scan when carrier lags

commits.author: usavsolutionsinc
commits.subject: fix(receiving): drop scanned boxes from Incoming via last-8 tracking match
commits.body:
Incoming only dropped a row when the Zoho-PO line advanced off EXPECTED, but a
dock scan/unbox writes to the carton's receiving_scans and never touches that
line (receiving_id is usually NULL), so scanned boxes stayed stuck in Incoming
and rendered as delivery_state='UNKNOWN'.

Read-side: add one SHIPMENT_SCANNED_PREDICATE — the last 8 of the Zoho-pasted
tracking number matched inside the scanned barcode digits (Zoho-pasted and
scanned values are different representations of the same package), plus a
shipment-link fallback. Apply it across the Incoming base, the DELIVERED_UNOPENED
facet/CASE, the summary chip counts + per-carrier CTE, and the canonical
delivered-unscanned tile, so a scanned box leaves Incoming the instant a scan
lands and count === rows.

Write-path twin: extend runTrackingMatchReconcileJob (reconcile-delivered cron)
with a D4 pass that advances a still-EXPECTED line to MATCHED + attaches it to
the scanned carton on the same last-8 match, so the line truly progresses in the
data model rather than only being hidden by the read-side guard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: drop scanned boxes from Incoming via last-8 tracking match

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: audit-trail: surface line-less label-print events in the SKU view (Q2)
commits.body:
ADMIN label prints and other serial-only lifecycle events (LABELED, GRADED,
PUTAWAY) carry ie.sku but no receiving_line_id, so they belong to no PO/line/
tracking and were dropped by the SKU view's inner JOIN on receiving_lines.

Decision: the SKU view is their home (they're SKU-scoped by nature; a synthetic
"unattached" bucket in the PO/Tech timelines would contradict the Line-under-PO
anchor). listSkus + getSkuDetail now LEFT JOIN receiving_lines and match on
COALESCE(rl.sku, ie.sku), so these events anchor to their SKU.

Q4 (keep tracking as a fallback anchor) needs no code change — getTechSessionDetail
already tries tracking -> shipment_id first, then falls back to the PO anchor.

Verified against the DB: SKU 00282 now surfaces 36 LABELED (+ GRADED/PUTAWAY)
events that the old inner JOIN dropped.

commits.title: audit-trail: surface line-less label-print events in the SKU view (Q2)

commits.body:
Deep-scanned the codebase for other status-enum lifecycle flows that follow the
FBA pattern (status column + ordered stages + a real UI page) and added them,
grounded in real code. Flows are now grouped into sections.

New flows:
  PO intake (Gmail triage) — email_missing_purchase_orders.pile:
    Inbox → Upload → Done (+Ignore); /api/admin/po-gmail/triage*
  Replenishment (purchasing) — replenishment_status enum (src/lib/replenishment.ts):
    Detected → Review → Plan PO → PO Sent → Awaiting Receipt → Fulfilled
    (+Cancelled; +pick-face REQUESTED→IN_PROGRESS→COMPLETE noted)
  Walk-in / local pickup — local_pickup_orders.status:
    Draft → Completed (Zoho PO + receiving) (+Voided)
  Cycle count — cycle_count_lines.status (src/lib/inventory/cycle-count.ts):
    Pending → Counted → Pending Review → Approved (+Rejected; campaign open/closed)
  Work assignments — assignment_status enum (work_assignments.status):
    Open → Assigned → In Progress → Done (+Canceled)

Grouped sections: Sourcing & intake, Outbound, Reverse & service, Inventory & ops.
OpsFlow gains group/order; display renders section headers with counts. Skipped
location_transfers / zoho_fulfillment_sync / stock_alerts (audit/internal, not
displayed lifecycles). tsc clean; build ✓.

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: workflow: flows display now mirrors the REAL codebase stages (deep scan)
commits.body:
Deep-scanned each flow's actual status vocabulary and rebuilt the descriptive
flows panel to match how the real pages display them — not generic lifecycle
states. Each step now carries the display label, the real status key, the
station, the activity/event signal, and the route that performs the transition.

FBA (fba_shipment_status_enum · src/lib/fba/status.ts):
  Planned(PLANNED, FNSKU_SCANNED, /api/tech/scan)
  → Tested("ready to go", /api/fba/items/ready)
  → Packed(/api/fba/items/scan)
  → Combined(LABEL_ASSIGNED — UI label is "Combined" — /api/fba/labels/bind)
  → Shipped(/api/fba/shipments/mark-shipped); +Out of Stock / Closed

Receiving (inbound_workflow_status_enum · workflow-stages.ts):
  Incoming → Scanned → Matched → Unboxed → Awaiting Test → Testing → Passed
  → Done; +Failed / RTV / Scrap

Outbound (order_unit_allocations.state):
  Allocated → Picked → Packed → Labeled → Shipped; +Released, carrier
  Accepted→In Transit→Delivered, and the Awaiting/Pending/Shipped UI buckets

Repair (repair_service.status, free-text): Incoming Shipment → Pending Repair
  → Awaiting Parts → Repaired, Contact Customer → Awaiting Pickup/Payment
  → Picked Up/Shipped; +Cancelled

Returns/RMA (rma_authorizations.status): Authorized → Received → Dispositioned
  (ACCEPT/HOLD/RTV/REWORK/SCRAP) → Closed; +Expired/Canceled, orders_exceptions

Display adds: per-flow source file, numbered step rail, real status-key chips,
signal + route annotations, and a "branches & terminal states" footer. Still
read-only (interactivity deferred). tsc clean; build ✓.

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: workflow: engine core — contract, registry, router, runtime, advance (Phase B)
commits.body:
Pure-TypeScript node engine, no existing code touched. All collaborators are
injected so routing/advance are unit-testable without DB or Ably:

  contract.ts  NodeDefinition/NodeContext/NodeResult + WorkflowStore boundary
  registry.ts  node-type registry (register/get/list, palette meta)
  router.ts    selectNextTarget — pure conditional routing over edges
  runtime.ts   runNode — times a node, maps thrown errors to the `error` port
  advance.ts   advanceItem — lock → run → record → emit → route/park/done
  store.ts     Drizzle WorkflowStore; passes organizationId EXPLICITLY because
               the neon-http client can't see the app.current_org GUC
  events.ts    emitWorkflowEvent → existing publishDbEvent (no-ops w/o Ably)
  index.ts     production wiring + registerBuiltins (nodes land in Phase C)

The concurrency lock is an injectable AdvanceLock (NULL_LOCK default); a real
Upstash lock gets wired when the live advance route lands in Phase D.

Tests: 13 passing (router + advance: linear, fail→repair branch, terminal,
await-park, thrown error, unknown type, context merge, not-enrolled, terminal
no-op, held-lock no double-advance). Added `npm run test:workflow`. tsc clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: workflow: engine core — contract, registry, router, runtime, advance (Phase B)

commits.subject: Zendesk claim: archive photos to NAS via office agent + warn on failure
commits.body:
The claim archive previously wrote to the local filesystem, which silently
no-ops on Vercel (no NAS mount). Route it through the office archive agent
over the tunnel when NAS_AGENT_URL/TOKEN are set (fall back to the direct
filesystem write on a LAN/dev box). Surface a toast warning in the claim
result when the archive is skipped/partial/fails, so a claim whose photos
didn't archive is no longer a silent success.

commits.subject: test(e2e): parts auto-sort spec (grade→PARTS sorts to TECH-PARTS bin)
commits.body:
Authenticated Playwright API spec covering the For-Parts auto-sort:
  1. seed a throwaway serial, grade PARTS → unit STOCKED in TECH-PARTS bin
     (parts_sorted:true), then delete the seed (zero residue)
  2. committed unit (ALLOCATED/SHIPPED) graded PARTS → parts_sorted:false,
     location unchanged
  3. PATCH a MATCHED receiving line to PARTS → needs_test cleared
Each test self-skips when data is absent and restores state in finally so it's
idempotent against the live DB.

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: feat(receiving): auto-sort "For Parts" units to the Technical Room parts bin
commits.body:
When a serial unit's condition is set to PARTS, it skips testing and the claim
flow and is auto-sorted into a pickable parts bin (STOCKED, so it stays sellable
and usable as repair stock). Fires from the grade endpoint (inventory unit panel
+ receiving per-chip condition menu) and from a receiving line's condition
(clears needs_test so the line drops out of the test queue, then sorts its
serials). Guards skip units already committed to an order/shipped or already in
the bin; failures never fail the grade/PATCH write.

- src/lib/inventory/parts-sort.ts: resolvePartsBin + sortSerialUnitToParts + guard
- serial-units/[id]/grade: hook on PARTS grade, returns parts_sorted
- receiving-lines PATCH: condition=PARTS -> needs_test=false + sort serials
- inventory/panels/UnitDetailsPanel: Parts - Tech Room badge + sort toast
- migration 2026-06-02_parts_bin.sql: seed pickable TECH-PARTS bin

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: feat(receiving): pair NAS photos to a PO from the desktop photo strip
commits.body:
Adds a "Select from NAS" control to the receiving PO photo strip
(ReceivingPhotoStrip, shown in the desktop carton/staff card). It opens a
picker dialog that browses the self-hosted NAS (Caddy), multi-selects
recent photos, and pairs them to the receiving package by URL via the
existing POST /api/receiving-photos { receivingId, photoUrl }.

Photos then render in the same PhotoGallery, support delete (DELETE
/api/photos/:id), and flow into Zendesk claims like any receiving photo —

commits.author: usavsolutionsinc
commits.subject: fix(receiving): label tracks selected unit condition on multi-qty lines
commits.body:
A PO line with >1 of the same item splits into per-unit rows
(ReceivingUnitRows), each with its own condition grade. But the label
preview/print used the line-level `cond`, so it always showed the line
default (rendered as "New" when empty) regardless of which unit was
selected — there was no per-item label.

ReceivingUnitRows now reports the selected unit's effective grade
(saved → pending → line default) via onActiveConditionChange, re-arming
the emit on line switch. LineEditPanel feeds that into the label payload's
conditionCode for multi-qty lines, so selecting an item refreshes the
label's condition; single-qty lines are unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: label tracks selected unit condition on multi-qty lines

commits.author: usavsolutionsinc
commits.subject: fix(auth): assign staff_roles on create/invite + honor persistent sessions
commits.body:
Two production auth bugs:

1. Packer/role FORBIDDEN: effective permissions resolve only from
   staff_roles×roles, but POST /api/admin/staff and /staff/invite wrote
   staff.role without inserting a staff_roles row — created staff resolved
   to an empty permission set and 403'd on every gated route while the UI
   still showed their role. Both endpoints now insert the matching
   staff_roles assignment (transactional) + invalidate the role cache;
   invite also canonicalizes/validates the role key.

2. Persistent login not sticking: signin bound sessions to shift end and
   createSession took min(shiftEnd, policyWindow), collapsing the
   persistent 1-year window to midnight; /api/auth/session slid the DB
   expiry but never re-issued the cookie, so the browser dropped it on
   lid-close/next day. persistent staff now bypass shift-bound expiry, and
   the heartbeat re-issues usav_sid with the slid max-age each call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: assign staff_roles on create/invite + honor persistent sessions
